skill.name: youtube-influencer-finder-api-skill
skill.description: Extract YouTube influencer profiles including subscriber counts, social links, and channel stats using the BrowserAct API.
skill.summary: Find and extract structured YouTube influencer data via an external API.
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: The skill makes legitimate HTTPS requests to the documented BrowserAct API and reads the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable. All network usage is expected for an API-integrated skill. The static analyzer flagged markdown code blocks in SKILL.md as external commands and generic API key patterns as high-risk, but these are false positives in context. No malicious intent was found.

low_findings.title: Third-party API dependency
low_findings.description: The skill sends keywords and an upload date filter to the BrowserAct API. Users should trust BrowserAct with their search terms and API key.

low_findings.title: Environment variable required
low_findings.description: The script reads BROWSERACT_API_KEY from the environment. Users must set this variable before running the skill.

content.user_title: Find YouTube influencers with the BrowserAct API
content.value_statement: Finding the right YouTube influencers for campaigns takes hours of manual searching. This skill automates discovery by querying the BrowserAct API and returning structured profiles with subscriber counts, emails, and social links.
actual_capabilities: Search YouTube influencers by keyword and upload date filter
Return structured profiles with subscriber counts, views, and video counts
Extract contact emails and visible social links when available
Poll long-running tasks and stream status logs with timestamps
Handle API key errors and retry transient failures once
limitations: Requires a valid BrowserAct API key set in the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable
Results depend on BrowserAct API availability and data coverage
Tasks may take several minutes due to automated browser operations
Only supports the upload date filters provided by the API template

use_cases.title: Build an influencer outreach list
use_cases.description: Marketing teams input niche keywords and receive a list of creators with contact emails and social links for campaign outreach.

use_cases.title: Research competitor channels
use_cases.description: Brand managers discover top channels in their niche and compare subscriber counts, views, and content frequency.

use_cases.title: Find recent creators in a topic
use_cases.description: Content strategists filter by upload date to identify active creators who posted this week or month.

prompt_templates.title: Find influencers by keyword
prompt_templates.prompt: Find YouTube influencers for the keyword 'fitness coaching' who uploaded this month.

prompt_templates.title: Build an outreach list
prompt_templates.prompt: Find YouTube creators in the 'sustainable fashion' niche and compile a list with their subscriber counts, emails, and social links.

prompt_templates.title: Compare competitor channels
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for 'tech review' channels that uploaded this week. Return channel names, subscriber counts, total views, and video counts so I can compare them.

prompt_templates.title: Research with retry handling
prompt_templates.prompt: Find active 'gaming' YouTubers from this month. If the task fails for any reason other than an invalid API key, retry once and then report the exact error.

output_examples.input: Find YouTube influencers for 'openclaw' who uploaded this month.
output: Profile Description: Tech reviews and tutorials for developers.

output_examples.input: Search for 'tech reviewer' channels from this week.
output: Results: 8 profiles found with subscriber counts ranging from 10K to 2.1M.

best_practices: Set the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable before running the script
Use specific keywords to improve result relevance and reduce noise
Monitor terminal output for status logs instead of interrupting long-running tasks
anti_patterns: Running the script without configuring the API key
Interrupting the process during polling because it appears unresponsive
Retrying automatically when the error contains 'Invalid authorization'

faq.question: What is the BrowserAct API and why do I need a key?
faq.answer: BrowserAct provides automated browser workflows. The API key authenticates your requests so the service can run the YouTube influencer search template on your behalf.

faq.question: How do I get a BrowserAct API key?
faq.answer: Visit the BrowserAct Console at the integrations page linked in the skill documentation, copy your key, and set it as the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable.

faq.question: Why does the task take several minutes?
faq.answer: The API performs real browser automation to scrape YouTube search results. Polling is normal and the script prints timestamps so you can monitor progress.

faq.question: What data does the skill return?
faq.answer: It returns structured influencer profiles including subscriber count, total views, video count, registration date, country, channel link, email, social links, and profile description.

faq.question: Can I use this skill with Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill is compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. The agent will run the Python script and handle polling for you.

faq.question: What should I do if the task fails?
faq.answer: If the output says 'Invalid authorization', check your API key and do not retry. For other errors, the agent will retry once automatically before reporting the issue.
